steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question. The task is to emphasize the aim — the investigative goal — of Nakahara's research study, not its methods, background curiosity, or conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — As it orbited the Moon, the Kaguya satellite collected data that was later analyzed by cosmochemist Hiroshi Nakahara.: Focuses on Kaguya's data collection method, not on the purpose driving Nakahara's research. ✗
  • Choice B — Before 2008, Hiroshi Nakahara wondered if the Moon was receiving some of its oxygen from Earth.: Describes Nakahara's pre-study curiosity, not the formal aim of the research. ✗
  • Choice C — Cosmochemist Hiroshi Nakahara set out to determine whether some of the Moon's oxygen was coming from Earth.: "Set out to determine" directly expresses the investigative goal — the purpose that drove the study. ✓
  • Choice D — Hiroshi Nakahara's study determined that Earth is sending a small amount of oxygen to the Moon.: States the study's conclusion, not its aim. ✗
That is the correct answer.
